title: The Lamb Crucified Before the Foundation of the Earth
summary:
The sermon "The Lamb Crucified Before the Foundation of the Earth," based on Revelation 13:8, reflects on the eternal plan of God to redeem humanity through Jesus Christ. Before sin even entered the world, God ordained Christ as the Lamb to be sacrificed, demonstrating that salvation has always been at the heart of His purpose. Blood is foundational to redemption, as Hebrews 9:22 teaches, and this truth threads through Scripture.

Three key biblical events highlight this: the Passover, where lamb’s blood protected Israelite homes in Egypt; the dedication of Solomon’s Temple, consecrated with thousands of sacrifices; and the New Covenant, established by Jesus at the Last Supper through His own blood. These moments show that deliverance, God's presence, and eternal life are rooted in the shedding of blood.

In contrast, Cain’s rejected offering and the Tower of Babel reveal the futility of foundations built without reliance on God's provision through blood. Cain’s life became aimless, and Babel’s prideful project was scattered. Similarly, without Christ’s sacrifice, our lives are vulnerable to collapse.

Jesus' teaching about wise and foolish builders (Matthew 7) reminds us that only foundations built on Him can endure life’s storms. Faith in Christ, the Lamb slain before the world’s foundation, secures our salvation—not through our works but through His grace (Ephesians 1:4-5). The blood of Jesus not only forgives but empowers believers to live victoriously (Revelation 12:11).

Easter’s message of resurrection confirms that Jesus' sacrifice defeated death, securing our place in the New Jerusalem where we will live eternally with God. The sermon concludes with an invitation to accept Christ's sacrifice, repent, and be baptized in Jesus name, establishing a firm foundation on the unshakable rock of Jesus Christ, the Lamb of God. Amen.
